Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Great cuts make for more accurate quilts, and Angela is here to share some tips and tricks for quick and precise fabric cutting. Plus, she shares the best ruler sizes to have on hand.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Master bowyer Tom Turgeon joins George Vondriska in the workshop to show you how to make your own long-bow. They'll discuss the kits available on the market and demystify the terminology used when talking about bow making. Learn about the art and intricac
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop

Image.alt: Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop
Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop
Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop

Video.summary: George Vondriska shows you how to make an occasional table to provide a convenient place for drinks, TV remotes, or a decorative lamp. He demonstrates the unique process of ebonizing, an alternative to stain used to give wood a rich, dark finish. With attractive tapered legs and a ceramic tabletop, this table is a great project to advance your woodworking skills.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Occasional Table

Video.summary: Guests on this show share ways to celebrate friends and family. First, Kazan Clark creates a magnet board with bottlecaps. Next, Julie interviews Dina Wakley. Then, Stacy Julian shows how to make a family tree album. Last, Julie makes pop up place cards.
Video.title: Episode 9: All in the Family

Video.summary: Artists are known for taking an unconventional approach to their art – often introducing "non art" supplies to their work. First we look at stamped metal for inventive beads. Then Candie Cooper throws a curve and uses cookie tins for her project. Next Kate Richbourg throws out the loom for a lalique bracelet that is loom free. We'll finish up with artist Tammy Honaman as she manipulates right angle weave to turn it into shapes and beads.
Video.title: Unconventional Art

Video.summary: Use shapes in new ways. Modern Quilt Guild marketing director Heather Grant discusses the modern quilt aesthetic and modern quilt designs. Blogger Rossie Hutchinson joins quilter Elizabeth Dackson to talk about improv quilt events. Instructor Rebecca Kemp Brent uses the latest cutting and quilting technology to create a confetti star quilt design.
Video.title: Shapes

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Holy scrap. Quilting can get a little messy sometimes, especially when you're busting your scrap stash. But nothing's gonna stop Angela from making room for new fabric! Feel the same? Your stitching sidekick awaits. (She's even sharing the free quilt pattern!)
Video.title: Shattered Frames

Video.summary: Quilting just got better. Join Angela in her first episode as she stitches up her exclusive variable-star quilt design.
Video.title: Stars

Video.summary: WARNING: The following content depicts excessive charm square usage and may be too exciting for most quilters. In other words, Angela's ready to take on 200+ precuts, and the accompanying placement decisions.
Video.title: Star Squared

Video.summary: This four-patch variation with cornerstones is the talk of the town! Watch Angela as she plays with color and dot-to-dot quilting.
Video.title: Town Square

Video.summary: Angela creates a modern Southwestern-style quilt that's the perfect size to use as a blanket for summer picnics in the park.
Video.title: Modern Southwestern-Style Quilt

Video.summary: What do you get when you combine a rainbow quilt, a star quilt and a medallion quilt all into one? A fresh episode of "The Midnight Quilt Show," of course! Join Angela as she breaks down Y-seams, quilting with templates and beyond.
Video.title: Frequency Rainbow Star Quilt

Video.summary: CAUTION: Baby quilts are a total gateway drug! Just ask Angela: she's working this cute little number up for her newborn nephew. (Think you can stop at one? Don't say we didn't warn you!)
Video.title: Z Is for Zoo

Video.summary: What happens when a rail fence meets a log cabin? Angela Walters' modern, lush batik quilt, demonstrated here!
Video.title: Rail Fence

Video.summary: Rainbows come out after dark when Angela whips up a rainbow lone star quilt!
Video.title: Solar Flare

Video.summary: Welcome to the second episode of the Holiday Gift-Along! Your hostess with the mostest (thread), Angela Walters, is back with another gift-worthy project idea: a basic quilted zipper bag with boxed corners. Don't worry, making zippers isn't as scary as you think!
Video.title: DIY Quilted Zipper Pouch

Video.summary: Adding super details raises us from good to super! Angela Wolf begins with the next lesson in our A line skirt and adds suede cutouts for a brand new look. Then it's a shirt you can complete in an evening with unique pull up sleeves for fashion and cosplay versatility with Cheryl Sleboda.
Video.title: Super Hero Projects

Video.summary: Take a walk on the wild side with some unique projects on this episode. First, Emily Thompson creates a quilted tablet cover - a perfect beginner project. Then, Cheryl Sleboda, our cosplay expert, has a novel idea for cosplay or plain fun: winged feet, you'll love these fun additions to your shoes. Last, Angela Wolf shares an optional addition to the wrap dress and a built-in tank top.
Video.title: Novel Ideas

Video.summary: Mix up your jewelry-making routine. Designer Kate Richbourg warms up by mixing colors and shapes in her bead palettes. She works out her designs with beads of all sorts. Then, she cools things down with techniques for crimping and creatively finishing necklaces.
Video.title: Vary Your Routine

Video.summary: Keep creativity flowing with inspiration from colors. On-line jewelry-making host and crafter Vicki O'Dell makes quick, thoughtful, colorful gifts for everyone. Katie sets crystals into a cup chain cuff and bangle, then adds knotted leather accents for a one-of-a-kind look.
Video.title: Color and Creativity

Video.summary: Scrapbook Soup is the show for scrapbookers, paper crafters, card makers and anyone interested in preserving memories through photos.
Video.title: Scrapbook Soup

Video.summary: Add something new to your creative routine. Tool expert Joe Rotella uses today's latest cutting technology to create glitter applique. Host Julie Fei-Fan Balzer makes collages with acrylic skins and films. Studio artist Cynthia Thornton paints fashions and faces to create a charming fabric doll. Jane Dunnewold share another art tip.
Video.title: New to me

Video.summary: The ladies are going big by thinking small! From compact cables to cute mini skeins, this one's all about the tiny treasures that keep those needles clicking.
Video.title: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Image.alt: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Video.summary: The ladies are going big by thinking small! From compact cables to cute mini skeins, this one's all about the tiny treasures that keep those needles clicking.
Video.title: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Image.alt: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Video.summary: A line is an infinitely extending one-dimensional figure; one that has length but not width or thickness. In art lines are the basis that give form to a piece of art. They can be straight or curved, thick or thin, and raised or flat. Projects featuring lines: a modern art canvas with paint and masking tape; lines become three dimensional as we make a playhouse for dolls or action figures; continue with your art journal and use brightly colored pens and a special tool to make spirograph line drawings. Finally make an unbreakable stained glass plaque with foil.
Video.title: Lines

Video.summary: Freshen up your style statement. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs fashions a colorful ID bracelet from clay and makes a foxy felt animal pouch. Jenny weaves a trendy beaded bracelet of silver and gold. Then, using fabric and ribbon, Jenny creates a decorator-style memo board to stay organized.
Video.title: Stylish

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: Sewing Machine Feet from A to Z
Video.title: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.grandparentTitle: Vanilla & Beyond: Baking With Flavors First
Video.title: Cook & Create

Video.summary: CAUTION: Baby quilts are a total gateway drug! Just ask Angela: she's working this cute little number up for her newborn nephew. (Think you can stop at one? Don't say we didn't warn you!)
Video.title: Z Is for Zoo
